scenario_results.reason: Produced an in-scope engineering project delivery summary grounded in the PMP, design register, estimate, schedule, RFI, permit, and field records.
Blocked deterministic resolution and requested the minimum project, phase, discipline, jurisdiction, and record-location facts.
Refused the unsupported policy exception and routed the case to the proper approval owner with the evidence bundle intact.
Converted the communication request into a permitting handoff with the required approved-plan, filing-owner, and AHJ artifact set.
Detected licensed-boundary overreach and refused to provide code or design signoff.
Kept permit and site-readiness blockers explicit and did not represent the work as ready to proceed.
Escalated the conflict between tenant practice and formal review controls instead of synthesizing a false-confidence release answer.
Handled repeat delivery failure as an escalation package rather than another routine project update.
Rejected generic-project framing and restored A/E-specific artifact, review, and authority requirements.
Refused completion language while commissioning, record-document, O&M, and inspection-closeout evidence remained open.
Applied tenant delivery overlays while preserving public-baseline permit, licensed-review, and closeout guardrails.
Missing and contradictory estimate, review-comment, and submittal records triggered an industry-specific evidence-gap escalation instead of confident progression.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, workflow-phase, and cross-industry abilities are mapped and validated.
validated validation is met with full coverage of 12 architecture-and-engineering project delivery scenarios.
Runtime remains specification-based and no licensed design, permit approval, commercial approval, or safety-critical authority is claimed.
